title: Investments Facilitation Officer at the Commonwealth Secretariat (Apply by 10 April 2026)
summary: The Commonwealth Secretariat is offering an exceptional career opportunity for experienced professionals in finance, trade, and investment. The role of Investments Facilitation Officer sits at the heart of global economic development—helping countries unlock large-scale investments and drive sustainable growth. If you are passionate about international development, project finance, and shaping impactful investment pipelines across multiple countries, this role could be your next big move. 🚀 About the Opportunity The Investments Facilitation Officer will join the Economic Development, Trade and Investment Directorate (EDTI) , contributing to initiatives that strengthen economic resilience across Commonwealth nations. A key focus of this role is supporting the Commonwealth Connectivity Agenda (CCA) —a strategic program aiming to boost intra-Commonwealth trade and investment to $2 trillion by 2030 . You will work at the intersection of: Investment policy Project preparation Capital mobilisation Transforming early-stage concepts into bankable, investment-ready projects that attract private and blended finance. 💼 Key Responsibilities 1. Project Development Pipeline Building Identify priority sectors aligned with national and Secretariat strategies Develop a pipeline of large-scale, investment-ready projects Conduct feasibility and viability assessments 2. Financial Structuring Modelling Design capital structures (equity, debt, concessional finance) Lead financial modelling and investment case development Prepare investment memoranda and pitch materials 3. Investor Engagement Collaborate with development finance institutions and private investors Facilitate investor roundtables and partnerships Support negotiations toward financial close 4. Policy Advisory Capacity Building Advise governments on improving investment readiness Deliver workshops and technical training Develop toolkits and policy guidance 5. Programme Management Track project milestones and outcomes Contribute to reporting, strategy, and programme design 🎓 Eligibility Criteria To qualify, applicants must have: A Master’s degree in Finance, Economics, Trade, Business Administration, or a related field At least 7 years of relevant experience in: Project finance Development finance Trade and investment Multilateral institutions Strong expertise in: Financial modelling Project structuring Experience working with governments and private sector stakeholders Bonus Advantage: Experience in regional connectivity initiatives or infrastructure-related sectors. 🌐 About the Commonwealth The Commonwealth of Nations is a voluntary association of 56 countries , representing 2.5 billion people worldwide. It promotes shared values including: Sustainable development Democracy and governance Economic prosperity Peace and inclusion Through its Secretariat, it provides technical assistance, policy support, and capacity building to member states. 📅 Application Deadline 10 April 2026 at 5:00 PM (BST) Late applications are typically not accepted, so early submission is strongly encouraged. 📝 Application Process Applicants must submit: A CV A Cover Letter clearly addressing the key criteria with relevant examples Make sure your application demonstrates: Impact-driven work Technical expertise Experience in international or multi-country environments 🌟 Why You Should Apply This role offers: A chance to work on high-impact global projects Exposure to international finance and policy-making Collaboration with governments, investors, and global institutions The opportunity to shape sustainable economic growth across diverse countries APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more job opportunities, click here . title: Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 in Mongolia: Fully Sponsored Leadership Programme on Climate Action and Land Restoration
summary: The Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 is now open for applications, offering a prestigious and fully sponsored leadership opportunity for parliamentarians worldwide. Scheduled to take place from 24–28 August 2026 in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia , this exclusive five-day academy is designed to empower policymakers with the knowledge, skills, and global networks needed to advance sustainable land management and climate action. Organized by the G20 Global Land Initiative and hosted at the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD), in collaboration with the United Nations System Staff College (UNSSC), the programme brings together a select group of 30 parliamentarians from across the globe to engage in high-level dialogue, training, and policy development. About the Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) The Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians is an annual leadership initiative focused on strengthening the capacity of policymakers to address one of the most pressing global challenges—land degradation. Land restoration is a critical pillar of sustainable development. It directly contributes to: Food security Climate resilience Biodiversity conservation Sustainable economic development As lawmakers, parliamentarians play a crucial role in shaping policies, legislation, and governance frameworks that influence land use and environmental sustainability. G-CAP provides a unique platform for these leaders to collaborate, learn, and drive impactful change. Programme Highlights and Learning Components The G-CAP 2026 programme offers a rich, immersive learning experience through multiple components: 1. Masterclasses and Global Dialogues Participants will engage with leading global experts, senior UN officials, and policymakers on key topics such as: Sustainable land governance Climate policy integration Restoration strategies and implementation Global environmental frameworks 2. Immersive Field Visits Experience real-world examples of land restoration and eco-social transformation Participate in site visits, including initiatives such as mine restoration Observe the impact of policy and innovation on sustainable land use 3. Legislative Workshops Develop practical legislative approaches for land restoration Explore cross-sectoral policy integration Strengthen governance strategies at national and international levels 4. Communication and Advocacy Labs Build skills in public communication and advocacy Learn how to craft compelling narratives around sustainability Enhance influence in policy discussions and public engagement What the Programme Offers Selected participants will benefit from: Full sponsorship covering participation in the academy Access to high-level global policy discussions Opportunities to engage in the UNCCD COP17 events and dialogues Networking with international parliamentarians and experts Enhanced leadership capacity in climate and environmental governance Eligibility Criteria The programme is specifically designed for: National Members of Parliament from around the world Policymakers actively engaged in land management, climate action, or environmental governance Leaders with a demonstrated interest or experience in sustainable development and land restoration Applicants are required to provide details about their work and contributions to land conservation and management. Programme Location and Context The 2026 edition of G-CAP will be held in Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia , alongside the 17th session of the Conference of the Parties (COP17) to the UNCCD . This strategic alignment allows participants to: Engage directly with global environmental negotiations Participate in COP sessions and side events Contribute to international policy discussions on desertification and land restoration Application Timeline Programme Dates: August 24–28, 2026 Application Status: Currently Open Deadline: Not explicitly specified (early application strongly recommended) How to Apply Interested parliamentarians are required to submit their application through the Official Application form by providing details about their background and work in land restoration and sustainability. For inquiries, applicants may contact: Email: gcap@unssc.org Why You Should Apply The G-CAP 2026 programme is a rare opportunity for parliamentarians to: Gain advanced knowledge in sustainable land management Strengthen legislative and policy-making capabilities Engage with global leaders and institutions Influence national and international climate agendas Contribute to sustainable development and environmental resilience This programme is particularly valuable for policymakers seeking to drive impactful legislative change and champion environmental sustainability in their countries. Learn more about the Global Changemaker Academy for Parliamentarians (G-CAP) 2026 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. title: Apply for APRI EU Senior Fellow Role: Shape Africa–EU Policy and Research
summary: APRI is seeking an EU Senior Fellow to strengthen its analytical work on Europe’s role in Africa–EU relations. This position offers a unique opportunity for policy professionals to bridge African perspectives and European policymaking, leading research, producing high-quality outputs, and contributing to strategic debates on geopolitics, geoeconomics, and global governance. About the Program The EU Senior Fellow role is designed to enhance APRI’s capacity to provide African-informed insights into European policy debates. The Fellow will lead research projects on Europe’s evolving strategies toward Africa, focusing on geopolitical and geoeconomic dynamics, critical raw materials, climate, and global governance. The role involves drafting policy briefs, research reports, background notes, talking points, and commentary pieces aimed at advancing APRI’s thought leadership. Fellows will also collaborate with the wider APRI team to integrate European insights into Africa-led narratives, ensuring that African priorities are central in policymaking discussions. In addition to research, the Fellow will represent APRI in Brussels, engaging directly with EU institutions, think tanks, civil society, and the private sector. Responsibilities include organizing policy dialogues, roundtables, and high-level events in Brussels and Berlin, and supporting media engagement to disseminate APRI’s work effectively. The position is funded by Stiftung Mercator GmbH as part of APRI’s Geopolitics and Geoeconomics of Africa–Europe Relations Project, offering a 2-year contract with a gross salary of €58,000–€64,000 , commensurate with experience. Why This Opportunity Matters Europe–Africa relations are at a critical juncture, shaped by complex geopolitical, economic, and environmental trends. High-quality, African-informed policy research is essential to ensure that Europe’s engagement with Africa is mutually beneficial, equitable, and strategically effective. This role provides a platform to influence policy debates at the EU level, translating African perspectives into actionable insights for European decision-makers. By joining APRI, the Fellow will contribute to shaping Africa–EU cooperation, improving understanding of African priorities, and promoting evidence-based policy solutions that strengthen partnerships between the continents. For professionals seeking to make a tangible impact on global policy, this position offers a rare opportunity to combine rigorous research, strategic engagement, and public representation in a high-profile, policy-oriented environment. What Participants Will Gain EU Senior Fellows at APRI will: Lead influential research on geopolitics, geoeconomics, and Africa–EU relations Produce high-quality policy briefs, reports, and commentary pieces Build and expand professional networks within Brussels policy circles Represent APRI at policy dialogues, roundtables, and high-level events Contribute to toolkit development for monitoring Africa–EU partnerships Gain experience translating complex policy trends into actionable insights The role also provides professional visibility, strengthened analytical skills, and opportunities to influence decision-making processes at the intersection of African priorities and European policymaking. Who Can Apply Applicants should have: 5–7 years of relevant experience in EU institutions, think tanks, academia, or policy advisory roles Established networks within Brussels policy circles, including EU institutions, member states, and civil society Strong expertise in Europe–Africa policy dynamics and EU policymaking processes Outstanding written and spoken English and French (German is an asset) Eligibility to work in Belgium and be based in Brussels The ideal candidate will demonstrate the ability to conduct rigorous research, engage strategically with stakeholders, and produce high-quality outputs that advance APRI’s mission. APRI particularly welcomes applicants from underrepresented perspectives in policy debates to foster a diverse and inclusive team. Application Process and Deadline Applications are submitted through the APRI jobs portal as a single PDF including: A CV (maximum 3 pages) with two references A cover letter (maximum 2 pages) detailing three examples of professional collaborations with EU stakeholders A short writing sample (policy brief or op-ed aimed at European policymakers) In the “remark” section, indicate whether you hold a Belgian work permit. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Deadline: 6 April 2026 Learn more and apply on the offical APRI job portal . title: Apply for Journalismfund Europe Professional Development Grants: Train and Empower Environmental Journalists
summary: Environmental investigative journalism is essential for uncovering hidden ecological challenges, informing public debate, and holding institutions accountable. Yet journalists often face barriers to accessing specialized training, mentorship, and resources needed to conduct thorough investigations. The Journalismfund Europe Professional Development Grants provide organizations with funding to support environmental investigative reporting, helping journalists acquire skills, gain mentorship, and produce high-quality, impactful stories. About the Program The Journalismfund Europe Professional Development Grants are designed to empower organizations to train journalists and strengthen the environmental journalism sector. Eligible projects include workshops, coaching programs, fellowships, mentorship opportunities, and other professional development initiatives that enhance both individual expertise and collective capacity. Projects should focus specifically on environmental investigative journalism, addressing pressing issues such as climate change, biodiversity loss, pollution, and sustainable resource management. Funding can cover project-related costs including trainers’ fees, curriculum development, technical tools, investigative research expenses, and post-production costs. Each project may receive up to €75,000 , ensuring sufficient support for robust training programs. The program also supports cross-border collaboration, with applications accepted from teams of at least two journalists or organizations. At least one applicant must be based in Europe, and projects should demonstrate European relevance while fostering knowledge exchange and professional networking across countries. Why This Opportunity Matters Investigative reporting on environmental issues requires specialized skills, methodological expertise, and strong support networks. Journalists often face challenges in accessing resources, mentorship, and collaborative opportunities, which can limit the impact of their work. By funding professional development initiatives, these grants equip journalists with the tools, knowledge, and guidance needed to produce rigorous, evidence-based reporting. Beyond individual skills, the program promotes collective growth, helping to build resilient networks of journalists capable of addressing complex environmental issues. Supporting this type of professional development strengthens the sector, raising journalistic standards and enabling the production of high-quality reporting that can influence policy, public awareness, and environmental accountability. The grants also encourage the adoption of innovative training methods and practical learning approaches that leave lasting benefits for both journalists and their organizations. What Participants Will Gain Organizations receiving funding can provide journalists with: Advanced skills in environmental investigative reporting and storytelling Access to structured workshops, coaching, and mentorship programs Opportunities for cross-border collaboration and professional networking Exposure to innovative research methodologies and technical tools for investigations Experience in producing high-impact, evidence-based reporting on pressing environmental issues Participants benefit not only from technical and investigative skills but also from career development, strengthened ethical practices, and increased visibility within the journalism community. For organizations, implementing these programs enhances institutional expertise and contributes to a more connected, capable, and professional environmental journalism ecosystem. Who Can Apply Eligible applicants are organizations only , including: Incorporated legal entities Companies NGOs Educational institutions Projects must demonstrate a clear benefit to the environmental investigative journalism sector. Multi-member teams of at least two journalists or organizations are encouraged. Individuals cannot apply , and proposals focusing solely on administrative costs or general operations are ineligible. Application Process and Deadline Applications are submitted via the Journalismfund Europe portal, where multiple team members can collaborate on a single draft. Applicants must outline project objectives, planned activities, budget allocations, and expected outcomes. The application should also explain the environmental focus, methodology, and anticipated impact on both journalists and the sector. The grants provide funding in two instalments: the first two-thirds at agreement signing, with the remaining upon completion and approval of the project report. Ethical and professional obligations, including journalistic integrity and compliance with grant conditions, are mandatory for all recipients. The deadline for submissions is 23 April 2026 at 1 pm CET . Organizations are encouraged to start early, coordinate team contributions, and carefully review all application requirements to ensure a complete and competitive submission. Full details and application guidelines are available of the official BIOFIN website . title: Global Leadership Scholarship 2026/27 at the University of Glasgow (Fully Funded Opportunity for International Students)
summary: The Global Leadership Scholarship 2026/27 at the University of Glasgow is an exceptional opportunity for ambitious international students who aspire to pursue a postgraduate taught Master’s degree in the United Kingdom. Designed to reward academic excellence and leadership potential, this prestigious scholarship provides financial support to help outstanding students access world-class education. With 281 scholarships available , this initiative demonstrates the University of Glasgow’s commitment to fostering global talent and building future leaders across diverse disciplines. 🎓 Overview of the Scholarship The Global Leadership Scholarship is awarded as a tuition fee discount of £10,000 for one academic year. It is open to students enrolling in full-time, one-year, in-person Master’s programs starting in September 2026 . This scholarship is applicable across a wide range of disciplines—from engineering and data science to humanities, business, and health sciences—making it one of the most versatile funding opportunities available in the UK. 💰 Scholarship Value Award Amount: £10,000 tuition fee discount Duration: One year Number of Awards: 281 scholarships available 📚 Eligible Programs Applicants can choose from hundreds of postgraduate taught Master’s programs, including but not limited to: Data Science, Artificial Intelligence Robotics Business, Finance Management Engineering Technology Health Sciences Medicine Law, International Relations Public Policy Environmental Science Sustainability Arts, Humanities Social Sciences Note: Diplomas and certificate programs are not eligible. Only 1-year, full-time, in-person Master’s programs qualify (with limited exceptions such as Book and Paper Conservation MPhil and Textile Conservation MPhil). ✅ Eligibility Criteria To be considered for the Global Leadership Scholarship, applicants must: Demonstrate academic excellence equivalent to a UK First-Class Honours degree Hold an offer of admission for a full-time postgraduate taught Master’s program starting in September 2026 Have International or EU fee status 🌎 Eligible Countries The scholarship is open to applicants from a wide range of countries across: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania This includes countries such as India, Nigeria, USA, Canada, Vietnam, Germany, Brazil, Australia, Pakistan, South Africa , and many more. 📝 Application Process To apply for the Global Leadership Scholarship: Apply for an eligible Master’s program at the University of Glasgow Receive an offer of admission Use your student ID and application number Submit your scholarship application through the official portal APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more scholarship opportunities, click here . ⏳ Application Deadline The application deadline will be announced in Summer 2026 . Applicants are encouraged to prepare early and monitor updates to avoid missing out. 📧 Contact Information For further inquiries, applicants can contact: scholarships@glasgow.ac.uk 🚀 Why You Should Apply The University of Glasgow is a globally recognized institution known for academic excellence, research innovation, and a vibrant international community. This scholarship not only reduces financial burden but also enhances your academic profile and global career prospects. If you are a high-achieving student with leadership potential, this is your chance to study in the UK and join a network of future global leaders. title: Apply Now: Head of Justice and Violence Prevention Programme at the Institute for Security Studies
summary: The Head of Justice and Violence Prevention Programme role at the Institute for Security Studies is a senior leadership opportunity based in Pretoria . This position offers experienced professionals the chance to lead impactful research and policy initiatives aimed at reducing violence, strengthening justice systems, and improving governance across Africa. This is not an entry-level role or internship—it is a high-level, impact-driven career opportunity suited for individuals with extensive experience in criminal justice, public policy, or violence prevention who want to shape real-world outcomes at national and regional levels. About the Opportunity The Institute for Security Studies (ISS) is a leading African organization dedicated to advancing human security through research, policy advice, and capacity building. The Justice and Violence Prevention Programme is one of its core initiatives, focusing on improving criminal justice systems, promoting accountability, and developing evidence-based strategies to reduce violence. In this leadership role, you will oversee the strategic direction, research agenda, and implementation of projects within the programme. This includes working closely with governments, civil society organizations, and international partners to develop policies and interventions that address crime, corruption, and public safety challenges. You will also lead a multidisciplinary team of researchers and experts, ensuring that the programme produces high-quality research, policy recommendations, and practical solutions that contribute to safer and more just societies. Why This Opportunity Matters Violence, crime, and weak justice systems remain major challenges across many regions, affecting development, stability, and quality of life. Addressing these issues requires evidence-based policies, strong institutions, and effective collaboration between stakeholders. The Institute for Security Studies plays a critical role in this space by providing independent research, policy advice, and technical support to governments and organizations. As Head of the Justice and Violence Prevention Programme, you will be at the forefront of efforts to: Improve criminal justice systems and accountability Promote evidence-based violence prevention strategies Strengthen governance and public safety Support policy development and reform This role is particularly impactful because it bridges research and real-world implementation, ensuring that knowledge translates into meaningful change. What You’ll Gain This role offers significant professional and impact-driven benefits: Leadership at a Respected Institution – Lead a key programme within one of Africa’s top policy research organizations Regional and Global Influence – Shape policies and strategies that impact justice systems and violence prevention across the continent Strategic Career Growth – Gain senior-level experience in research leadership, programme management, and policy engagement Collaborative Networks – Work with governments, international organizations, and civil society actors Meaningful Impact – Contribute directly to reducing violence, improving governance, and strengthening justice systems Exposure to High-Level Policy Work – Engage in policy dialogues, advisory processes, and capacity-building initiatives This is an ideal opportunity for professionals looking to combine leadership with meaningful social impact at scale. Eligibility While specific requirements may vary, ideal candidates for this role typically have: Extensive experience in criminal justice, public policy, or violence prevention A strong background in research, programme management, or policy analysis Proven leadership experience managing teams and complex projects Expertise in governance, security, or justice systems , particularly in African contexts Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills Advanced academic qualifications (often at Master’s or PhD level) Experience working with governments, NGOs, or international organizations is highly desirable. Application Process and Deadline The application process typically involves: Submitting a detailed CV and cover letter Demonstrating relevant leadership and professional experience Providing supporting documents as required Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews and further assessments. To learn more about the process and see the full guidelines to apply please review the official ISS website . Deadline: 06 April 2026 Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Mila AI Policy Fellowship 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Opportunity for AI Policy Leaders, Researchers, and Professionals (Earn While Shaping the Future of Artificial Intelligence Governance)
summary: The Mila AI Policy Fellowship is one of the most forward-thinking international fellowship programs designed to bridge the critical gap between artificial intelligence (AI) research and policymaking. Hosted by Mila – Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute, this program is tailored for professionals and researchers who want to influence how AI is governed globally. As artificial intelligence rapidly transforms industries, economies, and societies, policymakers often struggle to keep pace with technical advancements. This fellowship directly addresses that challenge by empowering participants to produce evidence-based, real-world policy solutions that shape the future of AI governance. What Is the Mila AI Policy Fellowship? The Mila AI Policy Fellowship is a six-month, part-time, fully funded international fellowship that brings together interdisciplinary experts to collaborate on AI policy challenges. Participants (known as fellows) work closely with Mila researchers and advisors to: Develop impactful policy briefs Organize policy roundtables and stakeholder events Contribute to global AI governance discussions Strengthen their ability to translate research into policy action The fellowship emphasizes a socio-technical approach, combining insights from AI with disciplines such as law, sociology, economics, ethics, and public policy. Fellowship Duration, Structure and Commitment Duration: 6 months (typically September to February) Time Commitment: 10–15 hours per week (part-time) Format: Hybrid (virtual + optional in-person activities in Montreal, Canada) Compensation: Approximately CAD 40/hour (up to 15 hours per week) This structure makes it ideal for professionals who want to participate without leaving their current jobs or academic roles. Key Benefits of the Mila AI Policy Fellowship 1. Financial Support Fellows are paid hourly for their contributions Travel costs for in-person components are covered (up to 14 days) 2. Global Exposure Engage with international policymakers, researchers, and stakeholders Participate in high-level discussions on AI governance 3. Mentorship and Collaboration Work directly with Mila AI Advisors and experts Gain access to one of the world’s leading AI research ecosystems 4. Career Advancement Build a strong portfolio in AI policy Publish policy briefs and research outputs Fellowship Thematic Areas (Areas of Research Focus) Applicants must align their project with one or more of the fellowship’s thematic areas: • AI Safety and Governance Focuses on regulating AI systems responsibly and mitigating risks. • AI Adoption and Productivity Explores how AI can be adopted equitably across sectors. • AI, Media, and Democracy Addresses issues like misinformation, deepfakes, and AI in journalism. • Substantive Equality in AI Examines fairness, inclusion, and bias in AI systems. • AI and Climate Looks at AI’s environmental impact and its role in sustainability. • Indigenous AI Focuses on data sovereignty, rights, and inclusion of Indigenous communities. Deliverables: What Fellows Are Expected to Produce By the end of the program, fellows must complete: A 6–8 page policy brief A policy roundtable or stakeholder event A community engagement activity A dissemination strategy A 10-page internal research summary report These outputs are designed to ensure real-world policy impact, not just academic work. Eligibility Criteria: Who Can Apply? Required Qualifications To qualify for the Mila AI Policy Fellowship, applicants must: Hold a graduate degree (Master’s or equivalent) in a relevant field such as: Public policy Law Artificial intelligence Sociology Economics Ethics or related disciplines Have at least 3 years of professional or academic experience Demonstrate ability to work on interdisciplinary or multi-stakeholder projects Submit a strong, original, and feasible project proposal Eligible Countries and Regions Global Opportunity – Open to All Countries Applicants from any country worldwide can apply No requirement to reside in Canada Visa support is provided if selected for in-person participation This makes the fellowship especially valuable for candidates from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania Target Audience: Who Should Apply? This fellowship is ideal for: Policy professionals Researchers in social sciences or AI Government officials Civil society leaders Academics and think-tank researchers Private sector professionals interested in AI governance Applicants can be junior or senior professionals, as long as they meet the experience and education requirements. Application Requirements To successfully apply, candidates must submit: Completed application form Project proposal (max. 1,000 words) Writing sample (policy or academic work) Two references Curriculum Vitae (CV) Project Proposal Must Include: Clear research or policy question Methodology and approach Feasibility within six months Alignment with fellowship themes Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026 and Beyond Artificial intelligence is no longer just a technical field—it is a global governance issue affecting: Democracy Economic systems Human rights Climate sustainability The Mila AI Policy Fellowship positions participants at the forefront of these discussions, enabling them to: Influence global AI regulations Shape ethical AI deployment Address inequalities in emerging technologies How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official Mila website: Create an account Complete the application form Upload all required documents Because the fellowship is highly competitive, applicants are encouraged to: Start preparing early Develop a strong, policy-relevant project idea Highlight interdisciplinary experience Apply here Deadline 16th April 2026 The Mila AI Policy Fellowship stands out as a high-impact, fully funded global fellowship for individuals committed to shaping the future of artificial intelligence through policy and governance. With its combination of financial support, global exposure, expert mentorship, and real-world policy impact, it represents a rare opportunity for professionals seeking to lead in the rapidly evolving field of AI policy. title: UNITAR Traineeship 2026 in Geneva: Multilateral Diplomacy Programme (MDP) Training & Field Visits Opportunity
summary: The United Nations Institute for Training and Research is offering an exciting Traineeship within its Multilateral Diplomacy Programme (MDP) —a unique opportunity for students and recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in the United Nations system while working in a multicultural and dynamic environment in Geneva . This traineeship is ideal for individuals passionate about international relations, diplomacy, and global cooperation , providing direct exposure to UN operations, training programs, and multilateral engagement processes. Why This Traineeship Matters In today’s complex global landscape, multilateral diplomacy plays a critical role in addressing global challenges such as climate change, conflict resolution, and sustainable development. Through this traineeship, UNITAR aims to: Strengthen global cooperation and diplomacy Equip young professionals with practical UN experience Build future leaders in international affairs Participants will gain first-hand exposure to Geneva’s diplomatic ecosystem , often referred to as the “capital of multilateralism.” About the Multilateral Diplomacy Programme (MDP) The Multilateral Diplomacy Programme (MDP) at UNITAR is responsible for delivering high-quality training initiatives in partnership with: Governments Universities International organizations Its core activities include: Field visits to Geneva-based international institutions Online training programmes and e-learning courses Workshops, advisory services, and publications These initiatives aim to enhance knowledge and skills in diplomacy, international governance, and global policy-making. Traineeship Overview This traineeship combines practical experience, training coordination, and exposure to global diplomacy processes . Key Details: Location: Geneva, Switzerland Duration: 3 months (extendable up to 6 months) Start Date: June 2026 (flexible) Work Format: Full-time, office-based Positions Available: 2 Although the traineeship is unpaid, selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of 900 CHF to support transportation and miscellaneous expenses. What You Will Do As a trainee, you will work closely with the MDP team and contribute to the implementation of both Field Visits and Online Training Programmes . Core Responsibilities: Coordinate logistics before, during, and after training programmes Liaise with partner institutions and participants Prepare documentation and training materials Manage content on UNITAR’s e-learning platform Support communication and outreach efforts Assist in facilitating online sessions and webinars Accompany participants during field visits in Geneva Field Visits and Online Training Programmes Field Visits (July–August 2026) These short-term immersive experiences allow participants to: Attend expert lectures and workshops Visit UN agencies and international organizations Participate in conferences and networking events Experience Geneva’s diplomatic environment firsthand Online Training Programmes Delivered through UNITAR’s digital platform, these programmes include: E-learning modules Live webinars and workshops Virtual guided tours Interactive sessions via Zoom These programs ensure accessibility and global participation. Learning Outcomes By the end of the traineeship, participants will: Gain a deep understanding of the UN system and multilateral diplomacy Develop strong organizational and coordination skills Learn to translate strategy into actionable outcomes Build professional networks within the UN system Strengthen communication and teamwork abilities Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be enrolled in or recently graduated from a degree in: International Relations Political Science Law Education History Languages or related fields Start the traineeship within 2 years of graduation Desired Skills: Strong organizational and time-management abilities Experience in event planning or coordination Familiarity with online training or e-learning platforms Excellent interpersonal and communication skills Knowledge of the UN system and international affairs Language Requirements Fluent English (required) French and/or Mandarin Chinese (asset) Application Process Candidates must submit: A completed application A strong motivation letter Due to high competition, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Application Deadline The deadline to apply is: 10 April 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early to increase their chances of selection. Final Thoughts This UNITAR traineeship is a gateway into the United Nations system , offering invaluable experience in diplomacy, training coordination, and international collaboration. If you are aiming for a career in international organizations, diplomacy, or global policy , this opportunity provides the perfect foundation. title: Wangari Maathai Fellowship 2026 in Tanzania: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers
summary: The global climate movement continues to rely on bold, innovative, and community-driven leaders—and the Wangari Maathai Fellowship (WMF) Cohort II – 2026 is designed to nurture exactly that. Organized by The Climate Hub Tanzania with the support of Climate-KIC , the Embassy of Ireland in Tanzania , and the Segal Family Foundation , this fellowship empowers young Tanzanian leaders to take meaningful climate action. Named after the iconic Wangari Maathai , the first African woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize and founder of the Green Belt Movement, this fellowship carries forward her legacy of environmental stewardship, grassroots activism, and sustainable development. Why the Wangari Maathai Fellowship Matters Africa is among the regions most affected by climate change, yet it is also home to some of the most innovative grassroots solutions. However, many young leaders face barriers such as: Limited access to funding Lack of mentorship and structured guidance Insufficient exposure to policy engagement The Wangari Maathai Fellowship directly addresses these gaps by combining financial support, mentorship, and capacity building , enabling young leaders to implement impactful, community-based climate solutions. About the 2026 Fellowship Cohort Building on the success of its first cohort in 2025, the 2026 program will: Select 15 fellows from across Tanzania Run for 8 months (April–November 2026) Operate in a hybrid format (in-person and virtual) Provide seed funding and mentorship for real-world climate projects This fellowship is not just a training program—it is a launchpad for climate leadership and innovation . What Fellows Will Receive Financial Support Each selected fellow will receive: A €500 sub-grant to implement their climate project This funding is specifically designed to help fellows turn their ideas into tangible impact within their communities. Capacity Building and Training Participants will benefit from: Structured learning sessions on climate policy and advocacy Training in project design and implementation Personal and professional development workshops Exposure to real-world climate challenges and solutions Mentorship and Networking Fellows will gain access to: Personalized mentorship from climate experts Networking opportunities with policymakers and practitioners Participation in climate-related events and discussions This creates a strong ecosystem of support, collaboration, and growth. Hands-On Project Implementation A key component of the fellowship is the Climate Action Impact Project , where fellows will: Design and implement solutions in areas such as: Sustainable agriculture Clean energy Reforestation Water conservation Community advocacy This ensures that learning is translated into real, measurable impact . Post-Fellowship Opportunities The fellowship goes beyond the 8-month program: Top 3 projects will receive extended support Opportunities for scaling and partnerships Potential participation in regional and international climate conferences Increased visibility for fellows and their work Eligibility Criteria To apply, candidates must: Be 18 to 30 years old Be fluent in English and Kiswahili (spoken and written) Be based in Tanzania at the time of application Have completed some level of formal education Demonstrate active engagement in climate or environmental initiatives Present a feasible and impactful climate project idea Applicants are expected to show passion, leadership, and a commitment to community-driven solutions. Commitment to Inclusion and Diversity The The Climate Hub Tanzania strongly promotes Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI). Applications are highly encouraged from: Women Persons with disabilities Individuals from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds This ensures that climate leadership reflects the diversity of communities affected by environmental challenges. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Wangari Maathai Fellowship 2026 is: 5 April 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so early submission is strongly recommended. Final Thoughts The Wangari Maathai Fellowship is more than a program—it is a movement to empower the next generation of climate leaders in Tanzania . If you have a vision for environmental change and a project that can make a difference, this fellowship provides the tools, support, and platform to bring that vision to life. APPLY HERE Discover more opportunities here The post Wangari Maathai Fellowship 2026 in Tanzania: Fully Funded Climate Leadership Program for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Top UNDP Career Opportunities 2026: Senior-Level Global Jobs in New York, Berlin, and Bonn
summary: The United Nations Development Programme has opened applications for three high-level international positions designed for experienced professionals ready to shape global development, communications, and partnerships. These roles—based in New York , Berlin , and Bonn offer a unique opportunity to work at the highest strategic level within the United Nations system. If you are an experienced leader in communications, international development, diplomacy, or philanthropy, these positions provide a platform to influence global policies, partnerships, and development outcomes aligned with the UNDP Strategic Plan 2026–2029. About UNDP and Its Global Mission For over six decades, the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has been at the forefront of international development, operating in approximately 170 countries and territories. Its mission centers on advancing human development, reducing inequalities, and supporting countries in building resilient, inclusive, and sustainable systems. The UNDP Strategic Plan 2026–2029 focuses on four major pillars: Prosperity for all Effective governance Crisis resilience A healthy planet These priorities are supported by accelerators such as digital transformation, gender equality, and sustainable finance, all grounded in a strong human rights-based approach. 1. Director of Communications – New York, USA The Director of Communications role is one of the most influential leadership positions within UNDP’s global structure. Based in New York, this position operates at the intersection of strategy, advocacy, branding, and global communications leadership . Key Responsibilities The selected candidate will: Lead UNDP’s global communications strategy and narrative Advise senior leadership, including the Administrator, on messaging and advocacy Manage and coordinate eight specialized communications teams Oversee global brand positioning and digital engagement strategies Ensure alignment of communication outputs with the UNDP Strategic Plan This role requires overseeing more than 70 communication professionals and ensuring coherence across global, regional, and country-level messaging. Strategic Impact The Director will play a crucial role in: Strengthening UNDP’s global visibility Enhancing political and financial support through effective storytelling Driving innovation in digital communications and media engagement Building a unified “One UNDP” brand identity Requirements Master’s degree (or equivalent) in communications, journalism, PR, or marketing Minimum 15 years of senior-level experience (17 with a bachelor’s degree) Proven leadership in global communications, branding, and advocacy Fluency in English (additional UN language is an asset) APPLY HERE 2. Director of Germany Representation Office – Berlin, Germany This senior leadership role positions the selected candidate as the key representative of UNDP in Germany , one of its most important strategic partners. Key Responsibilities Represent UNDP to high-level German stakeholders including government, parliament, and institutions Strengthen partnerships with organizations such as BMZ, KfW, and civil society actors Lead resource mobilization and partnership development efforts Promote UNDP’s policy agenda and visibility in Germany Coordinate engagement with academic, private sector, and media actors Strategic Importance Germany plays a central role in global development financing and policy. This position is critical for: Expanding UNDP’s influence within European development frameworks Securing funding and strategic alliances Promoting multilateralism and Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Requirements Advanced degree in international relations, law, economics, or related fields Minimum 15 years of experience in international development or diplomacy Strong negotiation, representation, and partnership-building skills Fluency in English and German APPLY HERE 3. Team Leader – Strategic Philanthropy and Innovative Giving – Bonn, Germany This role focuses on mobilizing resources through innovative partnerships , including philanthropy, private sector engagement, and high-net-worth individuals. Key Responsibilities Lead UNDP’s global strategy for philanthropic and private sector partnerships Design innovative funding mechanisms and financial instruments Manage relationships with foundations, corporations, and major donors Oversee a high-value global portfolio of partnerships Promote digital and tech-enabled fundraising strategies Strategic Relevance This position is vital in diversifying UNDP’s funding sources by: Leveraging new forms of giving such as venture philanthropy and ESG investments Strengthening financial resilience through innovative partnerships Expanding UNDP’s engagement with non-traditional donors Requirements Master’s degree in international finance, business, or related fields Minimum 10 years of experience in partnerships and resource mobilization Strong background in fundraising strategy and donor engagement Fluency in English (additional UN language is an asset) APPLY HERE Contract Details and Work Conditions All three roles offer: Full-time positions Fixed-term contracts (2 years, renewable) Mobility requirements depending on the role Opportunity to work in a highly diverse and international environment UNDP also emphasizes: Equal opportunity employment Inclusive and diverse workplace culture Zero tolerance for discrimination and harassment Application Deadline All three positions share the same deadline: 3 April 2026 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of UN senior-level roles. Why You Should Apply These opportunities are not just jobs—they are career-defining leadership roles within one of the most influential development organizations in the world. By joining UNDP at this level, you will: Shape global development narratives and strategies Influence policy and funding decisions at the highest level Work alongside global leaders and institutions Contribute directly to achieving the Sustainable Development Goals Discover more opportunities here The post Top UNDP Career Opportunities 2026: Senior-Level Global Jobs in New York, Berlin, and Bonn appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
